steps.title: Find BC using the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Triangle $ABC$ has a right angle at $C$, so $AB$ is the hypotenuse. With $AB=\sqrt{61}$ and $AC=6$, apply the Pythagorean theorem:
$$BC^2 + 6^2 = (\sqrt{61})^2$$
$$BC^2 + 36 = 61$$
$$BC^2 = 25$$
$$BC = 5$$

steps.title: Establish similar triangles ABC and AED
steps.content: Triangles $ABC$ and $AED$ are both right triangles and share angle $A$, so they are similar by AA similarity.

steps.title: Apply the similarity ratio to find the area
steps.content:
The scale factor from $ABC$ to $AED$ is:
$$k = \frac{AE}{AC} = \frac{6 + 18}{6} = \frac{24}{6} = 4$$
The height scales by the same factor, so $$ED = 4 \cdot BC = 4 \cdot 5 = 20.$$ Then the area of triangle $ADE$ is $$\frac{1}{2} \cdot AE \cdot ED=\frac{1}{2}\cdot 24\cdot 20=240.$$
